messages.content: I absolutely love TypeScript. The type system saves me from so many bugs.
Yeah it's been a game-changer for the JS ecosystem.
TypeScript generics are starting to drive me crazy. I spent 2 hours fighting with conditional types yesterday.
Generics edge cases can definitely be painful.
I've come around on this — TypeScript is fine for big projects with multiple people, but for quick scripts I'd just use Python now. Saves the type-juggling overhead.
That's a pretty common arc. Right tool for the job.
Also vegetarian by the way, in case it ever comes up for restaurant suggestions.
Got it, I'll keep that in mind.
